newsSinceJun6: No material news since June 6. CEO succession (Olivier Biebuyck replacing Yves Kerstens) was announced May 8 and took effect June 1 - already in the price. June 5 share buyback update (47,727 shares bought May 28-Jun 3, treasury at 4.13% of shares outstanding) is routine. Today's -1.69% move is ordinary drift with no identifiable catalyst.

sources.label: Bekaert - CEO succession announcement (GlobeNewswire, May 8 2026, effective Jun 1)
Bekaert - Update on Share Buyback Program and Liquidity Agreement (Jun 5 2026)
NV Bekaert SA (BEKB.BR) quote - Yahoo Finance

verdicts.rankRationale: No new AI-chain evidence since June 6; the CEO change (effective Jun 1) and routine buyback prints don't touch the latent steel-wire/reinforcement-to-electrification pathway. Modest YTD (+5.3%) and a small -1.7% day mean the latent-node setup is unchanged - hold, matching yesterday's verdict.
verdicts.bull: Under-owned EUR 2.3B Belgian industrial with steel wire, ropes and reinforcement exposure that could quietly feed datacenter construction, grid electrification and enclosure demand; ongoing buyback (4.13% treasury) shrinks the float while the market ignores the AI-adjacent pathway.
verdicts.neutral: A diversified rubber-reinforcement and steel-wire business whose AI linkage remains speculative; no contract, capacity or guidance datapoint since June 6 confirms or refutes the latent thesis, so the score should simply carry.
verdicts.bear: The AI pathway is thin - core earnings are tied to tire reinforcement and cyclical construction, not compute; a new CEO transition adds execution uncertainty, and analyst coverage is lukewarm (neutral consensus, ~EUR 44 average target near spot).
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: First strategy communication or guidance from new CEO Olivier Biebuyck, or a concrete datacenter/grid-infrastructure contract win that hardens the electrification pathway (H1 results late July).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Latent AI linkage never materializes into orders while cyclical tire/construction end-markets soften, leaving a low-growth industrial with no AI optionality premium.
